name: Electrical and Electronics Engineering
description: A comprehensive program integrating electrical power systems, electronics, control systems, and renewable energy. Prepares students for careers in power generation, transmission, distribution, and industrial automation.

subjects.name: Electrical Circuits
subjects.description: Fundamentals of DC and AC circuit analysis including Kirchhoff's laws, mesh and nodal analysis, network theorems, and phasor analysis. Covers RLC circuits, resonance, and three-phase systems.
learning_outcomes: Analyze DC and AC circuits using mesh and nodal analysis
Apply network theorems to simplify complex circuits
Analyze three-phase balanced and unbalanced systems
Determine transient and steady-state responses of RLC circuits
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundational for all electrical engineering roles in power, industrial, and electronics sectors.

subjects.name: Network Analysis
subjects.description: Advanced circuit analysis techniques including graph theory, two-port networks, network functions, and filter design. Covers Fourier and Laplace transform applications to circuit analysis.
learning_outcomes: Apply graph theory to formulate circuit equations
Characterize two-port networks using impedance and admittance parameters
Design passive filters using network synthesis techniques
Analyze circuits in frequency domain using Laplace transforms
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for power system analysis, signal conditioning, and filter design in industrial applications.

subjects.name: Power Systems
subjects.description: Generation, transmission, and distribution of electrical power including load flow analysis, fault analysis, and power system stability. Covers per-unit systems and economic dispatch.
learning_outcomes: Perform load flow analysis using numerical methods
Analyze symmetrical and unsymmetrical faults
Evaluate power system stability under disturbances
Apply economic dispatch principles for optimal generation
subjects.industry_relevance: Core discipline for utility companies, grid operators, and renewable energy integration projects.

subjects.name: Electrical Machines
subjects.description: Operating principles, characteristics, and analysis of DC machines, transformers, induction motors, synchronous machines, and special motors. Covers speed control, starting, and braking methods.
learning_outcomes: Analyze equivalent circuits and performance of transformers
Design speed control systems for DC and AC motors
Evaluate efficiency and losses in electrical machines
Select appropriate machines for specific industrial applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Fundamental for motor manufacturing, industrial automation, traction systems, and renewable energy generation.

subjects.name: Power Electronics
subjects.description: Design and analysis of power semiconductor devices, converters, inverters, choppers, and cyclo-converters. Covers switching devices, commutation techniques, and power quality issues.
learning_outcomes: Design AC-DC, DC-DC, and DC-AC converters for given specifications
Analyze commutation circuits and switching losses
Implement PWM techniques for voltage and current control
Evaluate power quality issues in converter-fed systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for renewable energy inverters, UPS systems, EV chargers, and industrial power supplies.

subjects.name: Control Systems
subjects.description: Analysis and design of feedback control systems using classical and modern techniques. Covers transfer functions, state-space representation, root locus, Bode plots, and PID controller design.
learning_outcomes: Model physical systems using transfer function and state-space methods
Analyze system stability using Routh-Hurwitz, root locus, and frequency response
Design PID and lead-lag compensators for desired performance
Implement control systems using analog and digital controllers
subjects.industry_relevance: Ubiquitous in industrial automation, process control, robotics, and aerospace systems.

subjects.name: Renewable Energy Systems
subjects.description: Solar, wind, biomass, hydro, and geothermal energy conversion systems. Covers grid integration, energy storage, power electronics interfaces, and policy frameworks for renewable deployment.
learning_outcomes: Design solar PV and wind energy conversion systems
Analyze grid integration challenges for renewable sources
Evaluate energy storage technologies for various applications
Perform techno-economic assessment of renewable energy projects
subjects.industry_relevance: Driving the global energy transition with massive growth in solar, wind, and battery storage industries.

subjects.name: High Voltage Engineering
subjects.description: Generation and measurement of high voltages, breakdown phenomena in gases, liquids, and solids, insulation design, and high voltage testing techniques. Covers lightning and switching surges.
learning_outcomes: Design high voltage generation and measurement circuits
Analyze breakdown mechanisms in different insulating media
Design insulation systems for power apparatus
Perform high voltage testing according to standards
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for power transformer, cable, and switchgear manufacturing and testing laboratories.

subjects.name: Electric Drives
subjects.description: Design and analysis of electric drive systems including DC and AC motor drives, speed control methods, regenerative braking, and four-quadrant operation. Covers converter-fed drives and vector control.
learning_outcomes: Design DC motor drives with chopper and converter control
Implement V/f and vector control for induction motor drives
Analyze four-quadrant operation of electric drives
Select appropriate drive systems for industrial applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for industrial automation, electric vehicles, traction systems, and renewable energy integration.

subjects.name: Smart Grids
subjects.description: Modern power grid concepts including smart metering, demand response, microgrids, distributed generation, and grid communication protocols. Covers cybersecurity and data analytics for grid management.
learning_outcomes: Design smart metering and advanced metering infrastructure
Analyze demand response strategies and their impact on grid stability
Design microgrid systems with distributed energy resources
Evaluate cybersecurity risks and mitigation strategies for smart grids
subjects.industry_relevance: Transforming traditional utilities with digital technologies for efficient and resilient power distribution.

subjects.name: Power Generation
subjects.description: Conventional and non-conventional power generation methods including thermal, hydro, nuclear, gas turbine, and combined cycle plants. Covers plant layout, efficiency analysis, and environmental impact.
learning_outcomes: Analyze performance and efficiency of various power plants
Design auxiliary systems for thermal and hydro power stations
Evaluate environmental impact of different generation methods
Apply cogeneration and combined cycle principles for efficiency improvement
subjects.industry_relevance: Core knowledge for careers in power generation utilities, plant operation, and energy consulting.

subjects.name: Transmission and Distribution
subjects.description: Design of transmission and distribution systems including line parameters, mechanical design, insulators, cables, substations, and distribution automation. Covers voltage regulation and power factor correction.
learning_outcomes: Design transmission lines considering electrical and mechanical parameters
Design distribution systems with proper voltage regulation
Plan substation layout and select appropriate equipment
Apply distribution automation techniques for reliability improvement
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for utility infrastructure planning, grid expansion projects, and smart distribution networks.

subjects.name: Electrical Machine Design
subjects.description: Design methodology for DC machines, transformers, induction motors, and synchronous machines including magnetic circuit design, thermal analysis, and optimization techniques.
learning_outcomes: Design magnetic circuits and windings for electrical machines
Perform thermal analysis and design cooling systems
Apply optimization techniques for machine design
Use CAD tools for electromagnetic simulation of machines
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for motor and generator manufacturing, electric vehicle traction motor design, and renewable energy generators.

subjects.name: Switchgear and Protection
subjects.description: Protective relaying, circuit breakers, and switchgear for power systems including overcurrent, distance, differential, and directional relays. Covers relay coordination and substation automation.
learning_outcomes: Design protection schemes for transmission lines, transformers, and generators
Select appropriate circuit breakers for various voltage levels
Perform relay coordination for radial and ring main systems
Implement substation automation using IEC 61850 standards
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for power system reliability, grid protection, and preventing equipment damage from faults.
